expectations.evidence: Assessment JSON at outputs/assessment.json follows the exact customer-review-assessor schema with all 3 perspectives, 5 criteria each, weights, and synthesis.

expectations.text: Assessment JSON output present with all three perspectives (reviewer, cso, market_expert)
expectations.evidence: assessment.json contains stakeholder_reviews array with perspective: 'reviewer' (score 100), 'cso' (score 86), 'market_expert' (score 100).

expectations.text: Each perspective has 5 scored criteria with pass/warn/fail and weights
expectations.evidence: Reviewer: role_completeness(0.30), pain_point_specificity(0.25), buying_criteria_realism(0.20), decision_dynamics_accuracy(0.15), deal_cycle_coherence(0.10). CSO: account_targeting_clarity(0.30), pain_to_proposition_mapping(0.25), objection_anticipation(0.20), multi_threading_guidance(0.15), named_customer_actionability(0.10). Market Expert: segment_calibration(0.30), vertical_authenticity(0.25), temporal_accuracy(0.20), information_source_validity(0.15), regional_accuracy(0.10). All have pass/warn scores.

expectations.text: Verdict is accept, revise, or reject
If verdict is revise, revision round executed (max 2)
expectations.evidence: Verdict was 'accept' on round 1, so no revision needed. Assertion is satisfied (conditional not triggered).

expectations.text: Final profile retains all required fields after revision
expectations.evidence: No revision was needed (accept verdict). Original profile preserved intact in outputs/grosse-energieversorger-de.json.
synthesis contains critical_improvements: [], high_improvements: [], optional_improvements: [2 items — CISO pain point refinement and named customer addition].

expectations.text: revision_guidance is non-empty when verdict is revise
expectations.evidence: Verdict was 'accept', not 'revise'. Conditional not triggered. revision_guidance is present and non-empty regardless ('Profile is strong across all three perspectives...').

suggestions.assertion: If verdict is revise, revision round executed (max 2)
suggestions.reason: This assertion is vacuously true when verdict is 'accept'. Need a separate eval where the input profile is deliberately weak to exercise the revision loop. Eval-29 (generate from scratch) is more likely to trigger revisions.
No assertion checks whether the scoring math is correct (e.g., CSO score of 86 = 100*0.30 + 60*0.25 + 100*0.20 + 100*0.15 + 60*0.10 = 30+15+20+15+6 = 86). Would catch scoring bugs.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass. The existing profile is strong enough that the revision loop isn't exercised. Eval-29 (generate) will provide better coverage of the revise path.
